steps.title: Move from impression to reveal
steps.content: The first sentence describes an initial impression: the photos may make viewers think of distant planets. The next sentence says the title brings viewers back to the reality of a frying pan, so the transition should signal the final turn in perception.

steps.title: Reject wrong relationships
steps.content: Choice A makes the title's effect a consequence of imagining planets. Choice B suggests an alternative, but both impressions occur in sequence. Choice D adds another similar point, while the sentence reverses the viewer's initial impression. The answer is C.
